name: Plateau pressure (Pplat)
description: Plateau pressure (Pplat) — airway pressure during an end-inspiratory hold, in cmH2O.

en.name: Plateau pressure (Pplat)
en.shortDescription: Plateau pressure (Pplat) — airway pressure during an end-inspiratory hold, in cmH2O.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Plateau pressure (Pplat) is the airway pressure measured during an end-inspiratory hold (no gas flow), reflecting the pressure distending the alveoli — the elastic recoil of the lungs and chest wall. It is a key target in lung-protective ventilation (kept ≤ ~30 cmH2O). In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, obtained by the ventilator during an inspiratory pause. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. It is reported in centimetres of water (cmH2O); some ventilators display mbar (1 cmH2O ≈ 0.98 mbar).

## Included Concepts

- *44782825 / 698822002 — Airway plateau pressure (SNOMED)*: the plateau pressure measured during an inspiratory hold (the default).
- *21490565 / 60802-6 — Dynamic plateau pressure (LOINC)*: a plateau pressure estimated under dynamic (non-hold) conditions.

**Note on units**: reported in **cmH2O** (`44777590 / cm[H2O] — centimeter watercolumn`, UCUM), the recommended unit, with a registered cmH2O↔mbar conversion (1 cmH2O = 0.98 mbar).

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when the source records a plateau pressure (Pplat): *44782825 / 698822002 — Airway plateau pressure*. Use *21490565 / 60802-6 — Dynamic plateau pressure* only when the source specifies a dynamic (non-hold) estimate.
- Convert mbar to cmH2O (×1.02) before mapping.
- **Not peak, mean or PEEP**: these are distinct pressures in their own concept sets.

fr.shortDescription: Pression de plateau (Pplat) — pression des voies aériennes lors d’une pause télé-inspiratoire, en cmH2O.
